details: node-ipc 12.0.1 ships an obfuscated module (node-ipc.cjs, hex-named identifiers such as _0xaed59b, _0x282d65, _0x4524e4 at line 1271) that, when loaded, resolves the host's public IP geolocation and, for hosts geolocated to specific countries, recursively overwrites files on the filesystem with a heart emoji. The payload fires as a side effect of requiring/loading the module inside any application that pulls node-ipc as a direct or transitive dependency (notably vue-cli and other widely-used tools). This is a destructive protestware payload: loading the package on a matching host destroys arbitrary files reachable by the running process, including files outside the package's own directory. The obfuscation (hex-mangled identifier names, string-array indirection) is consistent with deliberately hiding the destructive branch from casual review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
